heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: No stormy weather is anticipated.  The last big storm was recorded on 31 December 2025, lasting 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest dry periods recorded.  This dry period is ranked 4th since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 23 December 2025 and has lasted 10 days.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 0 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 0 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 18 July 2025, there have been only 62 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (202°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description: Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 5.3°C), outside temperature (up 5.1°C), and inside temperature (up 2.6°C).

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 33.8m lower than yesterday, 0.1m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 698m minimum and 1215m maximum, with an average of 956.1m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 956.1m and 956.0m.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; cold spells are likely. Also, the atmosphere will feel parched and dry and winds will be practically absent. The sky may alternate between sunny and overcast and moderate levels of sunlight will be present. Little change in the weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (5.0°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
